title: method_figure_brief — the ARIS upstream hand-off (paper-plan → method-figure)
description: The SEMANTIC brief paper-plan emits (after its claims_matrix) for a method/architecture figure. It decides WHAT the figure depicts; Step-0 turns it into a blueprint.json; method-figure renders+verifies. method-figure never invents anything not in this brief.

schema_version.description: optional but recommended — pins the brief contract paper-plan emits and compile_brief.py consumes

headline_claim.description: the contribution this figure must support

caption_thesis.description: the one-sentence thesis the figure should make a reader feel

topology_constraint.description: lock the layout logic so the renderer doesn't free-style the topology

one_line.description: one-line description shown in the box

visual_priority.description: core innovations get visual weight/center; background modules are quiet

identity_ref.description: id in identity_refs[] if a character/mascot lives here

symbol_registry.description: map every figure symbol to its paper/equation variable so they never disagree

identity_refs.description: the project's locked visual identities (created upstream, read-only); most papers have none

forbidden_tokens.description: terms the figure must NOT contain (avoid wrong/competing names)
